source: iteration 2 — ring segment click test failed: arcs invisible to the testing agent's interactive-element extraction
lesson: Clickable SVG parts need role=button + tabIndex=0 + key handler, and the ancestor svg must not carry role=img (it flattens the subtree to presentational).

source: iteration 3 — orbit-motion assertion failed: cloud browsers may run reduced-motion, and screenshot-sampled motion checks are flaky
lesson: Never cloud-assert continuous ambient motion; assert the settled end-state. Decoration animation is progressive enhancement, verified locally.

source: iteration 5 — runs returned status blocked while the agent's own conclusion said PASS with all steps green (platform quirk, reported upstream)
lesson: Audit the checker's verdict against its observations: a status flag that contradicts its own evidence gets one fresh run, then record what the evidence shows and report the discrepancy.

source: iteration 6 — the harness diagram's 11px 'pass' label was unreadable in the cloud tester's screenshots
lesson: Meaning-carrying labels in data graphics render at >=13px SVG (720 viewBox): if it's only legible because you know what it says, it isn't legible.

source: iteration 7 — the tester clicked the replay diamond ('Seek to iteration 2') instead of the ring segment; two controls shared the entity name
lesson: Plan steps must name the widget type and exclude same-entity look-alikes; accessible names for actions (Seek to...) and reveals (Open story card...) should diverge.

source: iteration 9 — per-segment intro timing made the ring invisible for 3+ seconds once the loop hit 8 iterations; the tester indexed mid-intro and found no clickable arcs
lesson: Cap the total duration of data-driven entrances (budget / item count, never per-item x count); interactive controls must be visible within ~1s no matter how much the data grows.

source: iteration 13 — the 'press ? for shortcuts' hint's aria-label ('Open keyboard shortcuts') overrode its visible text, hiding the '?' from the accessibility-tree-reading cloud tester
lesson: A control whose visible text is the information must not carry an aria-label that omits it — the label wins in the a11y tree and hides the text from agents and screen readers. Omit it or mirror the info. (Third a11y lesson: L1 hid controls, L5 confused names, L7 hid content.)

source: iteration 15 — the Loop Card's wordmark/tagline/stats were SVG text only, invisible to the a11y-tree-reading cloud tester (and the judge-CTA re-tripped L7 with a masking aria-label)
lesson: Content-bearing SVG needs a real text equivalent (an sr-only HTML block or role=img + full aria-label); decorative SVG gets aria-hidden. If an agent or screen reader can't read the graphic's words, neither can a judge on assistive tech. (Fourth a11y lesson.)
